impact_on_current_20260521_trace.reason_for_zero_impact: All 24 GPU wrfout T fields were detected as perturbation_from_300K_base, matching the WRF wrfout convention already used by v1.

theta_side_specific_canonicalization.new_behavior: WRF T is always converted by +300 K; GPU T is detected as absolute theta or perturbation theta before conversion.
theta_side_specific_canonicalization.old_behavior: The comparator added 300 K to T symmetrically for GPU and WRF without knowing whether GPU T was absolute or perturbation.

fields_that_appear_HEALTHY.evidence: RMSE 0.0 and max absolute difference 0.0 across 24 hours
RMSE 0.044 m s-1 despite max local difference 2.70 m s-1

method_artifacts_remaining_to_audit: T2 single-cell max difference remains unphysically high and needs field-level outlier localization
SWDOWN/GLW/HFX magnitudes remain physically suspicious but are not explained by units/stagger metadata in wrfout headers
method_artifacts_resolved: theta convention: resolved for this trace; GPU wrfout T and WRF wrfout T are both perturbation fields

evidence: HFX max absolute difference 4105 W m-2
primary_defects_ranked_by_likely_impact.hypothesis: surface heat flux magnitude/sign or state coupling wrong

evidence: SWDOWN 24h RMSE 510 W m-2 and max difference 1122 W m-2
GPU wrfout T convention is perturbation, same as WRF
max absolute theta difference remains 345.20 K
primary_defects_ranked_by_likely_impact.hypothesis: real theta divergence remains after convention audit

evidence: GPU PBLH is zero at hour 1 while WRF PBLH is nonzero
primary_defects_ranked_by_likely_impact.hypothesis: PBL diagnostic/MYNN output incomplete or wrong

evidence: LU_INDEX RMSE 2.6447 and max category delta 14 every hour

primary_defects_ranked_by_likely_impact.hypothesis: wind/pressure divergence downstream of theta, surface flux, and boundary completeness defects

method: hour-by-hour wrfout GPU-vs-WRF d02 across 24 forecast hours and 16 operational fields, after M9.C side-aware theta canonicalization
method_caveat: Still wrfout-level comparison, not per-operator WRF Fortran trace. Comparator now canonicalizes theta side-specifically; current GPU NetCDF T is detected as perturbation, so numerical results match v1 for theta.
recommended_next_dispatch_order: M11 real theta/dycore guard divergence using corrected comparator

theta_convention_audit.conclusion: The current GPU NetCDF writer stores T as WRF-compatible perturbation theta, not absolute theta. The comparator is now robust to either GPU convention, but the M9 v1 theta RMSE was not caused by a 300 K offset artifact.
theta_convention_audit.gpu_State_theta_convention: absolute potential temperature K in src/gpuwrf/contracts/state.py
theta_convention_audit.wrf_T_convention: perturbation potential temperature theta-t0; canonical theta = T + 300 K

verdict_evidence: Comparator theta convention is now trustworthy for both GPU absolute and GPU perturbation wrfout variants. The current Canary 20260521 trace still diverges materially, but the failures remain localized to planned M10-M14 work rather than a comparator-only offset artifact.
viability_caveats: 1000-step dycore parity not measured (codex sandbox GPU init blocker); manager-side rerun needed
The trace was a wrfout-level comparison not a per-operator-boundary trace; operator attribution is inferred from field signatures not directly observed
20260521 is a single case; the 30-case M20 ensemble will reveal how the defects vary across seasons
